evals.prompt: How do I build a multi-step welcome journey in Iterable for new app users?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide to creating a Studio journey with event-based entry, Send tiles for email and push, delays, and filter branching

assertions.name: mentions_studio
assertions.description: Response must reference Studio as Iterable's journey builder tool

assertions.name: entry_trigger
assertions.description: Response must explain setting up an event-based entry trigger (custom event or list subscription)

assertions.name: multi_channel_tiles
assertions.description: Response must include multiple channel Send tiles in the journey (e.g., email + push + in-app)

evals.prompt: How do I use Brand Affinity to improve my email targeting in Iterable?
evals.expected_output: Guide to using Brand Affinity labels for segmentation and journey branching

assertions.name: brand_affinity_labels
assertions.description: Response must mention the Brand Affinity label categories (Loyal, Positive, Neutral, Negative, Never Engaged)

assertions.name: segmentation_usage
assertions.description: Response must explain how to use Brand Affinity labels as segmentation filters or journey branch conditions

assertions.name: data_requirement
assertions.description: Response must mention that Brand Affinity needs sufficient historical engagement data (30+ days) to generate accurate labels

evals.prompt: How do I use the Iterable API to track a purchase event with line items?
evals.expected_output: API call example using POST /api/commerce/trackPurchase with items array

assertions.name: correct_endpoint
assertions.description: Response must reference POST /api/commerce/trackPurchase as the endpoint

assertions.name: api_key_auth
assertions.description: Response must show Api-Key header authentication

assertions.name: items_payload
assertions.description: Response must demonstrate the items array with id, name, price, and quantity fields
